defectsFoundAndFixedThisRun.issue: Timeline fill was a one-time 150ms-delayed, fixed-1.8s-CSS-transition boolean flip -- not actually linked to scroll position. Prior QA report (2026-07-14) incorrectly claimed genuine scroll-driven behavior.
defectsFoundAndFixedThisRun.fix: Rewrote using framer-motion useScroll(target: timelineRef)+useSpring+motion.i style scaleY; marker activation via single top-level useMotionValueEvent->activeCount state.
defectsFoundAndFixedThisRun.verifiedVia: next build clean; re-measured with corrected timeline-relative protocol: 0.1697 -> 0.5247 -> 0.9934, markers 1->3->6

defectsFoundAndFixedThisRun.issue: Duplicate marquee <span> items lacked aria-hidden=true in JSX (only hidden via reduced-mode CSS), unlike Homepage/Services duplicates.
defectsFoundAndFixedThisRun.fix: Added aria-hidden={dup||undefined} to duplicate spans in ToolRow(), blanked alt text on duplicate images.
defectsFoundAndFixedThisRun.verifiedVia: next build clean; runtime-confirmed duplicateAriaHiddenAllTrue=true, duplicateTabbableCount=0

priorRunDefects.issue: useInView result unused; scroll-trigger animation rendered pre-filled
duplicate non-dismissible announcement bar with dead #cta anchor

reason: All three marquees pass the full shared runtime contract. Process start/middle/end progression is genuinely scroll-linked and monotonic with recorded provenance (scrollY, timeline bounds, selectors). Homepage acceptance passes 16/17 required strings across all 3 viewports with the single miss explained as a spec-vs-source naming mismatch, not a missing feature; 7/7 forbidden strings absent. No console errors, no failed requests, no horizontal overflow across 27 checks.
